top_signals.title: China’s AI Ambitions Depend on Massive Power-Grid Investments
top_signals.summary: Beijing’s $295 billion data center rollout will need to be supported by continued spending on electricity generation and networks.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Kazakhstan Oil Buyers Demand More Supply as Hormuz Closure Tightens Market
top_signals.summary: Customers buying oil from Kazakhstan are urging the OPEC+ producer to ramp up crude supply and deliver the maximum available volumes, Kazakhstan’s Energy Minister Yerlan Akkenzhenov said on Wednesday. “In light of restrictions in the Strait of Hormuz, our partners are asking us to increase supplies,” Reuters quoted Akkenzhenov as telling reporters in Astana today. “They are asking for the maximum,” the minister said, but added that “Naturally, we have infrastructure constraints within the limits of Kazakhstan’s…
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: Iran and Israel say they have halted strikes on each other for now - Reuters
top_signals.summary: Iran and Israel say they have halted strikes on each other for now  Reuters

top_signals.title: Armenia’s pro-Europe party wins election despite Russia-linked disinformation
top_signals.summary: Pashinyan's Civil Contract party won nearly 50% of Sunday's vote, defeating the pro-Russian Strong Armenia party led by Russian-Armenian billionaire Samvel Karapetyan, which received around 23% of the vote.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Two reasons for optimism after Tuesday's whipsaw market sell-off
top_signals.summary: There's reason for optimism, judging by bullish options flows around stocks that do better when interest-rates stay lower, and call-buyers who are prepared for the biggest move in Oracle since Covid.
